Video.summary: Matt and Bruno take a stock TJ and make it into the machine it was born to be. Once we upgrade the Steering and Suspension, we’ll make some much needed repair.
Video.title: TJ Off-Road Upgrade

Video.summary: The 1970 Plymouth Superbird work is resumed, and the 1970 Charger R/T collision repair is completed. The 1970 Barracuda Convertible's 383 2bbl. V8 is assembled and painted.
Video.title: Manifold Destiny

Video.summary: A secret is accidentally revealed by Mark. Mark and Daren visit the local AAMCO Transmissions to watch the overhaul of the 1970 Barracuda Convertible's 727 Automatic Transmission, and then the Barracuda has its A/C box and instrument panel installed. The 1970 Superbird assembly nears completion.
Video.title: Spoiler Alert

Video.summary: Automotive parts supplier Tony D'Agostino of Tony's Parts helps the shop get organized, and Mark tests his Mopar knowledge against Tony's. Work on a 1970 Plymouth Superbird is completed.
Video.title: MoSparring

Video.summary: The 1970 Superbird's restoration and story is recapped, and the Hills visit the shop to take delivery of the car, along with its original owner. Mike and Michael Hill drive the Superbird on a cross county trip home to South Carolina.
Video.title: The Hills Have Eyes on a Superbird

Image.alt: The Hills Have Eyes on a Superbird
The Hills Have Eyes on a Superbird
The Hills Have Eyes on a Superbird

Video.summary: With the 1970 Plymouth Superbird done and safely driven home, Mark is asked to restore a 1969 Daytona Charger. Due to its condition, Mark questions if it's practical and if it's even legal.
Video.title: Clipped Wings

Video.summary: Just when things couldn't get more stressful, Mark agrees to finally get the 1930 Chevy Coach fully restored by the end of the week. Meanwhile, Mark QC's the next cars in the process of resurrection.
Video.title: Put Me In, Coach

Video.summary: See the complete 3-year restoration of Kimberly Cook's 1970 Barracuda Convertible: the body, paint, engine, and convertible top, culminating in Graveyard Carz' most emotional reveal to date.
Video.title: How to Cook a Barracuda

Video.summary: New shop. New crew. An All-new Graveyard Carz! The ghouls tackle a 1970 Cuda.
Video.title: Commando Cuda vs. Magnum Force

Video.summary: Mark paints a Hemi Charger in his favorite Mopar color, burnt orange. AMD delivers the Phantom Cuda, and the ghouls finish the Dailey's 1970 Cuda 383, a project four years in the making.
Video.title: Rallye Red Restored

Video.summary: The ghouls wrap up the 340 Cuda and install panels on the Hemi Charger. Mark trains Allysa on O.E.M. assembly line markings. The legendary red Plymouth Fury "Christine" visits the shop.
Video.title: Hell Hath No '58 Fury

Video.summary: The ghouls have problems with after-market parts for the 400 Magnum V8. Allysa begins working in the shop, and two rare Mopars are disassembled
Video.title: 400 Magnum Misfire

Video.summary: Mark's coffee addiction is spotlighted, and the ghouls work on a 1970 Challenger.
Video.title: Return of the '70 Sunroof Challenger

Video.summary: Work on a 1970 Cuda 340 is featured.
Video.title: 70 'Cuda Grace

Video.summary: The ghouls work on a 1968 GTX for Bill Goldberg, and Mark repairs a Challenger deck lid. Will paints a 1967 Hemi GTX and challenges Mark to a paint-off.
Video.title: Hemi Orange is the New Corporate Blue

Image.alt: Hemi Orange is the New Corporate Blue
Hemi Orange is the New Corporate Blue
Hemi Orange is the New Corporate Blue

Video.summary: Mark teaches Allysa to analyze and document cars in preparation for disassembly, but she later has a crucial lapse in judgement. The ghouls install the 400 Magnum in the 1972 Dodge Charger.
Video.title: Grand Theft Hemi

Video.summary: Two rare Mopars, a Daytona survivor and early Hemi Charger, are analyzed for authenticity and restoration.
Video.title: Investigation O.E.

Video.summary: The ghouls complete the 1970 V-Code Challenger and make a side-by-side comparison with Mark's 2014 Shaker-hood Challenger.
Video.title: Chally vs. Chally

Video.summary: A 1970 Hemi Charger is restored.
Video.title: HEMI FK5 Alive

Video.summary: The 1970 Hemi Charger project is completed in the season finale.
Video.title: Pitch and Rides

Video.summary: The Graveyard Ghouls return with the final assembly of the legendary General Lee which so happens to also be the official screen-used, record holding, freeway jump-car from the 2005 Dukes of Hazzard film.
Video.title: General Assemble Lee

Video.summary: Mark isolates himself in the machine-shop and begins the assembly of the 440 4BBL for the 1970 Plum Crazy Challenger previously owned by a former cast member.
Video.title: Put Up Your Dukes

Video.summary: It’s a delivery day at Graveyard Carz! We jump back and show you every moment in the restoration of our legendary, screen-used, record holding, General Lee from the 2005 Dukes of Hazzard film. It all builds to a reveal the Duke Boys would be proud of.
Video.title: General Deliver Lee

Video.summary: We bring our 2011 Cam Am Commander back for some final fixes. We beef up the steering with an electric power steering kit and throw some light on the subject with a large LED Light bar.
Video.title: Can Am Fix

Video.summary: This cool blue 54 Chevy shortbed is fresh out of the paint shop and we’ve been given the task of putting on the finishing pieces to the project. We lay in the wood bed, add some finishing components and this truck is ready for its first show.
Video.title: Paint Shop Chevy

Video.summary: Why do head gaskets keep failing on Ford 6.0 Powerstroke Trucks? The EGR Cooler is starting to fail. Matt and Bruno will show you how to delete the problem for good on Truck U.
Video.title: EGR Cooling Delete

Video.summary: It’s the case of wreck the truck and pass the buck. A 1990 Blazer broke on the trail, but who’s at fault? Matt and Bruno gather evidence and make a final ruling on Truck U.
Video.title: Blazer Trial
